response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior full-stack developer with strong Linux background, DLang systems work (low-latency async I/O, fibers at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby), and geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map). However, the role specifically requires modern C++ or Rust proficiency, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015), with no Rust experience. No demonstrated experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance, or large-scale distributed routing pipelines. The DLang systems work and geospatial domain knowledge are transferable, but the core language requirement is a significant gap.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map platform
Low-latency systems work in DLang with async I/O, fibers, and high-throughput services at Sociomantic
Strong Linux, testing discipline (TDD), and full-stack adaptability
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; C/C++ work is over 10 years old and limited in scope
No navigation guidance or routing pipeline experience
Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js, not systems-level performance engineering
